learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate understanding of the role of social media in the overall marketing strategy for the business.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of various social media platforms and strategies.
Explain different metrics and ways to measure performance to achieve tactics.
Design and implement a portfolio with various social media marketing strategies and/or tactics.
Identify, analyse and resolve social marketing management issues and risks.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment requires you to prepare a social media marketing campaign. You will need to be able to access a range of social media sites (e.g. Facebook, Instagram, etc.) to complete this assessment.

Individually, you are to choose a company and develop a social media marketing campaign for the company based on course learning. 

This assessment requires the following: 

  • 8 page report that identifies and discusses the company, campaign background and social media marketing strategy you are recommending for the company of your choice.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Must be submitted electronically via the link on Blackboard by the due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Google Garage Digital Marketing Completion and Reflection Paper
assessments.task_description:
This assessment involves a reflection on engagement with, and completion of, the Google Garage: Digital Marketing accreditation. 

The assessment requires the following:

  1. The completion of the Google Garage: Digital Marketing accreditation*.
  2. A reflection (800 words) on your engagement with the accreditation (using the template provided).
  3. Evidence of completion of the accreditation (see the template for details). 

*It is strongly recommended that the accreditation is completed progressively over the course of the semester, both in the seminar and independently. NOTE: Time will be allocated in the workshop sessions to complete some accreditation modules and discuss any issues, questions or concerns. Please attend classes knowing that the progressive completion of the Google Garage modules is an expectation; in doing so, you will be helping yourself to 'keep on track' with achieving our course objectives. 

Further details will be available in workshops and on Blackboard. A link to the accreditation and a template for the reflection will also provided on Blackboard.

You will need to be able to access Google Digital Garage to complete this assessment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Must be submitted electronically via the link on Blackboard Ultra by the due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: The final examination will focus on applying Social Media Marketing theories and frameworks based on weekly seminar content during the semester. Further information will be given in week 13 during class.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
